pages.full_text:
van with a view of the setting sun across the bay. There were 2 life guards in orange patrolling the beach on foot, + an inflatable motor boat with 4 more life guards patrolled the bay. Thomas + Peter practised Baywatch drill, falling out of the boat by the beach. Heaven help their victims. Charles help Eve collected seaweed for the allotment. John + Charles built sand castles on the beach. It is lovely, + not too crowded.
	Simon prepared food. The Spanish seem to have lunch at about 2.30. + supper at 10.

__Sunday__
	We swam + set off at 1.0. high at 2.45. We are going south along the coast. One village with ugly smokey factory chimneys. All the other villages very pretty small campings, small beaches + fishing villages. Rafts, 60 of them in one bay, probably with ropes hanging down for muscles to breed on. Muros would be the most interesting old village to stay in.
	We stopped for lunch by the sea
pages.notes: No handwritten page number visible at top-left; this is a continuation page. 'muscles' likely means 'mussels' (diary spelling preserved). 'Baywatch drill' refers to the TV show. The text is cut off at the bottom ('We stopped for lunch by the sea'). Year is 1989 based on surrounding pages; the Sunday date cannot be pinned precisely beyond August 1989. 'high' after '1.0.' likely refers to tide — 'high at 2.45'. 'campings' is the Spanish word for campsites.

pages.full_text:
horreos

By tea time at 5.30., we had turned inland. Maize grown everywhere. Almost every old farm house has its horreos.
	We crossed into Portugal, after Eve drove a lightening tour in Vigo. We had to drive until 9.50. pm to find the only campsite around - in __Braga__ *
	From the sublime to the ridiculous. We are parked on a spare corner of ground next to a pile of road gravel. Cheek by jowl throughout the campsite, but at least no one quite next to us. The wash house like an abattoire. But a glorious view, first of the sunset across the city, then of skyscrapers sparking in dawn sun - churches, working cranes.
	Because it was Sunday evening we have been unable to change [illegible].
pages.notes: Page number 15 visible at top-right. The page has a hand-drawn sketch of a horreos (a Galician/Portuguese raised stone granary structure on stilts with a cross on top), labelled 'horreos' at the top-right of the sketch. This is an illustration, not a route sketch. 'abattoire' is the diary's spelling of abattoir. 'lightening' is the diary's spelling (presumably meaning 'lightning-fast'). The final word '[illegible]' appears to be 'escudos' (Portuguese currency) but is not fully legible. The asterisk after 'Braga *' may indicate a footnote or annotation. Vigo is in Galicia, Spain — they crossed into Portugal after Vigo.
